description: secretx-k8s/src/lib.rs:171-228 and README.md:21-32. Two write paths with different semantics is more code than 'always SSA' or 'always merge', but it captures a real distinction: with-key writers expect to coexist with other writers on the same Secret (one writer per key), and merge-patch achieves that without per-key field-manager bookkeeping. No-key writers own the value, and SSA expresses that ownership in metadata.managedFields. Resist suggestions to unify to one strategy without engaging with the cohabitation requirement.
title: Distinguishing ?key= vs no-key with merge-patch vs SSA is a real design call
